articles.headline: India Launches Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Eyes Global Leadership
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and establishing export hubs.
• This phase targets a significant increase in green hydrogen production capacity by 2030, aiming for 10 million metric tonnes annually.
• Key initiatives include dedicated manufacturing zones, R&D incentives for electrolyser technology, and pilot projects for green steel and green ammonia.
• The mission seeks to reduce India's reliance on fossil fuel imports and position the country as a major global supplier of green hydrogen.
• Financial outlays include production-linked incentives (PLI) for electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production.

Investment models

articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all normal matter.

articles.headline: Supreme Court to Review Petitions Challenging Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court has listed several petitions challenging the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDPA), 2023, for an early hearing.
• Petitioners argue that certain provisions of the Act infringe upon fundamental rights, particularly the right to privacy and freedom of speech.
• Concerns have been raised regarding the powers of the Data Protection Board, exemptions granted to government agencies, and the definition of 'significant data fiduciaries'.
• The government maintains that the Act balances individual privacy with the need for data processing for legitimate purposes and national security.
• The outcome of these hearings could significantly impact India's digital economy and data governance framework.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.

mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional challenges posed to the DPDPA, 2023, particularly concerning the right to privacy.
Discuss the balance between state surveillance, national security, and individual privacy in the context of data protection laws.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the Data Protection Board in ensuring compliance and safeguarding citizen's data rights.
Examine the implications of the DPDPA on businesses, especially startups and SMEs, operating in the digital space.
Compare India's data protection framework with global standards like GDPR.

articles.trivia: The 'Right to be Forgotten' is a key provision in many data protection laws, allowing individuals to request the deletion of their personal data under certain conditions.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes with Focus on Maritime Security and Digital Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded with leaders reaffirming commitment to a comprehensive strategic partnership, emphasizing shared values and interests.
• Key outcomes include enhanced cooperation in maritime domain awareness, counter-terrorism, and disaster management in the Indo-Pacific region.
• New initiatives were announced to bolster digital connectivity, promote digital public infrastructure, and facilitate cross-border digital payments.
• Discussions also focused on boosting trade and investment, with a review of the ASEAN-India Free Trade Area (AIFTA) agreement.
• India reiterated its support for ASEAN centrality in the regional security architecture and peaceful resolution of disputes.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Persistent Inflationary Pressures
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at its latest meeting, citing persistent inflationary concerns.
• The MPC emphasized the need to remain vigilant against rising food prices and global commodity price volatility, which could impact domestic inflation.
• Projections for Q3 and Q4 2025-26 inflation were revised upwards, indicating a cautious stance towards monetary easing.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target of 4% while supporting economic growth.
• Analysts suggest that future rate cuts are unlikely until inflation shows a clear and sustained downward trajectory.

Suggest alternative or supplementary measures that could be adopted by the government to complement RBI's efforts in managing inflation.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building with a graph showing economic indicators in the foreground.
articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: National Quantum Mission: India Makes Strides in Quantum Computing and Communication
articles.explainer:
• India's National Quantum Mission (NQM) announced significant progress in developing indigenous quantum computing hardware and secure quantum communication networks.
• Researchers demonstrated a 64-qubit quantum processor prototype, marking a crucial step towards building larger-scale quantum computers.
• Pilot projects for quantum key distribution (QKD) have been successfully implemented in several metropolitan areas, enhancing cybersecurity infrastructure.
• The mission emphasizes collaboration between academia, industry, and government to foster a vibrant quantum technology ecosystem.
• Funding has been allocated for establishing Quantum Technology Hubs across the country to accelerate R&D and skill development.

articles.trivia: A quantum computer with just 300 qubits could theoretically store more information than the number of atoms in the observable universe.

articles.headline: PM-Vishwakarma Scheme Expands to Include New Artisanal Trades, Boosts Rural Economy
articles.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) announced the expansion of the PM-Vishwakarma scheme to include additional traditional crafts and trades.
• The scheme provides financial assistance, skill training, access to modern tools, and marketing support to artisans and craftspeople across India.
• The new trades include specific forms of traditional pottery, intricate weaving, and specialized metal crafts, aiming to preserve cultural heritage.
• This expansion is expected to further empower the Vishwakarma community, enhance their income, and integrate them into the formal economy.
• Focus remains on promoting 'Vocal for Local' and 'Made in India' initiatives by strengthening traditional Indian craftsmanship.

Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

articles.imageDescription: An Indian artisan meticulously working on a traditional craft, surrounded by his tools.
articles.trivia: The term 'Vishwakarma' refers to the divine architect, often associated with craftsmanship and creation in Hindu mythology.

articles.headline: India Boosts Climate-Resilient Agriculture Initiatives Amidst Erratic Monsoon Patterns
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare announced new measures to promote climate-resilient agriculture practices across vulnerable regions.
• Focus areas include developing drought-resistant crop varieties, precision irrigation techniques, and early warning systems for extreme weather events.
• The initiatives aim to mitigate the impact of increasingly erratic monsoon patterns and ensure food security for the growing population.
• Farmers will receive financial incentives and technical training for adopting sustainable farming methods like conservation tillage and agroforestry.
• Collaboration with agricultural research institutions and international bodies is being strengthened to share best practices and technological innovations.

articles.trivia: India is the world's second-largest producer of rice and wheat, making its agricultural sector highly vulnerable to climate shifts.

articles.headline: National Medical Devices Policy 2023: Progress on Domestic Manufacturing and Export
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare presented an update on the implementation of the National Medical Devices Policy 2023, highlighting increased domestic manufacturing.
• The policy aims to reduce import dependence, promote R&D, and establish India as a global manufacturing hub for medical devices.
• Several new medical device parks have become operational, attracting significant investments from both domestic and international players.
• Regulatory reforms are underway to streamline approval processes and ensure the quality and safety of domestically produced devices.
• India is also focusing on increasing exports of high-quality medical devices to various international markets.

articles.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Model Gains Global Traction; New e-Governance Initiatives Launched
articles.explainer:
• India's pioneering Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, encompassing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, is being adopted by several developing nations.
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched new e-governance initiatives to further streamline public service delivery.
• These include an integrated citizen service portal and AI-powered grievance redressal systems, aiming for greater efficiency and transparency.
• The DPI framework is seen as a cost-effective and scalable solution for financial inclusion and digital empowerment.
• International collaborations are expanding, with India sharing its expertise to help other countries build their own robust digital ecosystems.

articles.headline: Namami Gange Mission Enters Phase III: Focus on Tributary Cleaning and Public Participation
articles.explainer:
• The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) announced the commencement of Phase III of the Namami Gange programme, extending its scope.
• The new phase places increased emphasis on the rejuvenation of Ganga's tributaries, including the Yamuna, Gomti, and Kosi, which contribute significantly to pollution.
• Greater public participation and community engagement will be fostered through localized initiatives and citizen science projects.
• Advanced wastewater treatment technologies and bioremediation techniques will be deployed to tackle industrial and municipal pollution.
• The mission aims for a holistic approach, integrating sanitation, biodiversity conservation, and sustainable river basin management.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.

articles.trivia: The Ganga river basin is the most populous river basin in the world, home to over 500 million people.

articles.headline: India Champions Global South Development at UN Forum, Calls for Equitable Climate Finance
articles.explainer:
• India spearheaded discussions at a UN Development Forum, advocating for greater support and cooperation among countries of the Global South.
• Emphasized the need for equitable climate finance mechanisms, urging developed nations to fulfill their commitments to developing economies.
• Highlighted India's own development models and experiences in areas like digital public infrastructure, renewable energy, and public health, as replicable solutions.
• Called for reform of international financial institutions to better address the needs and voices of the Global South.
• India reiterated its commitment to South-South Cooperation, offering technical assistance and capacity building to partner countries.

articles.headline: Major Cities Roll Out Advanced Urban Flood Resilience Plans Ahead of Monsoon Season
articles.explainer:
• Several Indian metropolitan cities have initiated comprehensive urban flood resilience plans in anticipation of heavy monsoon rains.
• The plans involve upgrading stormwater drainage systems, implementing real-time flood monitoring, and developing early warning dissemination protocols.
• Focus is also on nature-based solutions such as creating urban wetlands, permeable pavements, and green infrastructure to absorb excess water.
• Public awareness campaigns are being launched to educate citizens on flood preparedness and emergency response measures.
• Collaboration between municipal corporations, disaster management authorities, and urban planning experts is key to these integrated strategies.

articles.headline: India Reaffirms Commitment to Indus Waters Treaty, Calls for Bilateral Dialogue on Technical Issues
articles.explainer:
• India reiterated its commitment to the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) with Pakistan but emphasized the need for bilateral resolution of technical disputes.
• The Ministry of External Affairs stated that routine consultations under the Permanent Indus Commission are crucial for addressing issues related to run-of-the-river projects.
• India highlighted its right to optimum utilization of its share of waters for agricultural and hydropower purposes, within the treaty's framework.
• Calls were made for Pakistan to engage constructively in dialogue to resolve long-standing differences over specific project designs.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank, is considered one of the most successful water-sharing agreements globally.

Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.

articles.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus River system with its tributaries, highlighting the treaty-allocated rivers.
articles.trivia: Despite multiple conflicts, the Indus Waters Treaty has largely remained intact, with very few interruptions in its implementation since its signing.

articles.headline: Archaeologists Unearth Evidence of Advanced Textile Production in Ancient Harappan Sites
articles.explainer:
• Recent archaeological excavations at a previously unexplored Harappan site in Gujarat have uncovered significant evidence of sophisticated textile production.
• Finds include well-preserved cotton fibers, spindle whorls, and dyeing vats, indicating advanced knowledge of textile manufacturing and trade.
• This discovery sheds new light on the economic activities and artistic skills of the Indus Valley Civilization.
• Analysis of the textile fragments suggests diverse weaving techniques and the use of natural dyes, showcasing a rich material culture.
• The findings further reinforce the idea of the Harappans as skilled artisans and traders with extensive networks.

articles.headline: Poshan Abhiyaan Shows Positive Trends in Reducing Malnutrition, Focus on Adolescent Health
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development released a progress report on the National Nutrition Mission (Poshan Abhiyaan), indicating significant reductions in stunting and wasting among children.
• The mission's multi-sectoral approach, integrating health, anganwadi services, and WASH (Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene), has yielded positive outcomes.
• The next phase will place increased emphasis on addressing malnutrition and anemia among adolescent girls and pregnant women.
• Technological interventions, including the Poshan Tracker app, have enhanced data collection and monitoring of nutritional indicators.
• Community-led initiatives and awareness campaigns remain central to achieving the mission's goal of a 'Malnutrition-Free India'.

articles.headline: India Accelerates Border Infrastructure Development in North-Eastern States
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs announced accelerated plans for border infrastructure development along India's North-Eastern frontiers.
• Projects include construction of all-weather roads, strategic bridges, and advanced surveillance systems to enhance security and connectivity.
• The move aims to bolster India's strategic presence, facilitate trade, and integrate remote border areas with the national mainstream.
• Special focus is on improving connectivity to Bhutan, Myanmar, and Bangladesh borders to foster regional economic cooperation.
• The initiative is part of a broader strategy to strengthen border management and counter cross-border illegal activities.

articles.headline: NEP 2020: Focus on Digital Education and Skill Integration in Higher Education
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education provided an update on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, highlighting progress in digital education.
• Initiatives include the expansion of online learning platforms, development of digital content in regional languages, and promotion of blended learning models.
• Special emphasis is placed on integrating vocational and skill-based courses into higher education curricula to enhance employability.
• The Academic Bank of Credit (ABC) system is being widely adopted to facilitate credit transfer and multidisciplinary learning.

articles.headline: Government Consults Stakeholders for India's AI Ethics and Governance Framework
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has initiated a broad consultation process for developing India's Artificial Intelligence (AI) ethics and governance framework.
• The framework aims to ensure responsible and ethical development and deployment of AI technologies, addressing concerns like bias, privacy, and accountability.
• Discussions involve experts from academia, industry, civil society, and legal fields to create a comprehensive and future-proof policy.
• The focus is on promoting 'AI for All' while safeguarding fundamental rights and preventing potential misuse of AI.
• This move aligns with global efforts to establish norms and standards for AI governance.

articles.headline: India Inaugurates New Mega Solar Energy Parks, Boosts Renewable Capacity

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
2. The mission targets a production capacity of 5 million metric tonnes per annum (MMTPA) by 2030.
3. The Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT) programme is a key component of the mission.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The mission's primary objective is to position India as a global leader in green hydrogen. Statement 2 is incorrect. The article mentions a target of 10 million metric tonnes annually by 2030 in Phase II. Statement 3 is correct. SIGHT programme (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) is a crucial component offering financial incentives.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following are key components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model?
1. Aadhaar
2. Unified Payments Interface (UPI)
3. DigiLocker
4. Open Network for Digital Commerce (ONDC)
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: All four options – Aadhaar (identity), UPI (payments), DigiLocker (documents), and ONDC (e-commerce) – are considered pillars of India's Digital Public Infrastructure model, facilitating various digital services and financial inclusion.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT):
1. The Treaty was brokered by the United Nations in 1960.
2. The Eastern Rivers (Sutlej, Beas, Ravi) are allocated for India's unrestricted use.
3. The Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) is responsible for implementing the Treaty.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; the IWT was brokered by the World Bank, not the United Nations. Statement 2 is correct, the Eastern Rivers are for India's unrestricted use. Statement 3 is correct, the PIC is the bilateral mechanism for implementation.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following best describes the primary objective of the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)?
options: (a) To achieve maximum employment in the economy.
(b) To maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
(c) To manage the government's fiscal deficit.
(d) To regulate foreign exchange markets and balance of payments.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) To maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The primary objective of the MPC, as mandated by the RBI Act, is to maintain price stability (inflation targeting) while considering the objective of growth. Other options relate to broader economic goals or specific RBI functions, but not the MPC's primary mandate.

prelimsMocks.question: Lothal, an ancient Harappan site, is renowned for which of the following?
options: (a) The discovery of a large number of unique terracotta figurines.
(b) Being the world's earliest known tidal dockyard.
(c) Evidence of advanced iron smelting techniques.
(d) The existence of a highly elaborate temple complex.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Being the world's earliest known tidal dockyard.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Lothal is most famous for its archaeological remains of a sophisticated dockyard, making it a crucial port city of the Indus Valley Civilization and the earliest known tidal dock in the world.

GS1.explainer:
• Several Indian metropolitan cities have initiated comprehensive urban flood resilience plans in anticipation of heavy monsoon rains.
• The plans involve upgrading stormwater drainage systems, implementing real-time flood monitoring, and developing early warning dissemination protocols.
• Focus is also on nature-based solutions such as creating urban wetlands, permeable pavements, and green infrastructure to absorb excess water.
• Public awareness campaigns are being launched to educate citizens on flood preparedness and emergency response measures.
• Collaboration between municipal corporations, disaster management authorities, and urban planning experts is key to these integrated strategies.

• The findings further reinforce the idea of the Harappans as skilled artisans and traders with extensive networks.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court to Review Petitions Challenging Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court has listed several petitions challenging the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDPA), 2023, for an early hearing.
• Petitioners argue that certain provisions of the Act infringe upon fundamental rights, particularly the right to privacy and freedom of speech.
• Concerns have been raised regarding the powers of the Data Protection Board, exemptions granted to government agencies, and the definition of 'significant data fiduciaries'.
• The government maintains that the Act balances individual privacy with the need for data processing for legitimate purposes and national security.
• The outcome of these hearings could significantly impact India's digital economy and data governance framework.

GS2.headline: India Reaffirms Commitment to Indus Waters Treaty, Calls for Bilateral Dialogue on Technical Issues
GS2.explainer:
• India reiterated its commitment to the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) with Pakistan but emphasized the need for bilateral resolution of technical disputes.
• The Ministry of External Affairs stated that routine consultations under the Permanent Indus Commission are crucial for addressing issues related to run-of-the-river projects.
• India highlighted its right to optimum utilization of its share of waters for agricultural and hydropower purposes, within the treaty's framework.
• Calls were made for Pakistan to engage constructively in dialogue to resolve long-standing differences over specific project designs.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank, is considered one of the most successful water-sharing agreements globally.

GS2.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus River system with its tributaries, highlighting the treaty-allocated rivers.
GS2.trivia: Despite multiple conflicts, the Indus Waters Treaty has largely remained intact, with very few interruptions in its implementation since its signing.

GS2.headline: Poshan Abhiyaan Shows Positive Trends in Reducing Malnutrition, Focus on Adolescent Health
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development released a progress report on the National Nutrition Mission (Poshan Abhiyaan), indicating significant reductions in stunting and wasting among children.
• The mission's multi-sectoral approach, integrating health, anganwadi services, and WASH (Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene), has yielded positive outcomes.
• The next phase will place increased emphasis on addressing malnutrition and anemia among adolescent girls and pregnant women.
• Technological interventions, including the Poshan Tracker app, have enhanced data collection and monitoring of nutritional indicators.
• Community-led initiatives and awareness campaigns remain central to achieving the mission's goal of a 'Malnutrition-Free India'.

GS2.trivia: India accounts for over a third of the world's stunted children, making it a critical area for public health intervention.

GS2.headline: NEP 2020: Focus on Digital Education and Skill Integration in Higher Education
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education provided an update on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, highlighting progress in digital education.
• Initiatives include the expansion of online learning platforms, development of digital content in regional languages, and promotion of blended learning models.
• Special emphasis is placed on integrating vocational and skill-based courses into higher education curricula to enhance employability.
• The Academic Bank of Credit (ABC) system is being widely adopted to facilitate credit transfer and multidisciplinary learning.
• Efforts are underway to bridge the digital divide in education, ensuring equitable access to online resources for all students.

GS3.headline: India Launches Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Eyes Global Leadership
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and establishing export hubs.
• This phase targets a significant increase in green hydrogen production capacity by 2030, aiming for 10 million metric tonnes annually.
• Key initiatives include dedicated manufacturing zones, R&D incentives for electrolyser technology, and pilot projects for green steel and green ammonia.
• The mission seeks to reduce India's reliance on fossil fuel imports and position the country as a major global supplier of green hydrogen.
• Financial outlays include production-linked incentives (PLI) for electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Persistent Inflationary Pressures
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at its latest meeting, citing persistent inflationary concerns.
• The MPC emphasized the need to remain vigilant against rising food prices and global commodity price volatility, which could impact domestic inflation.
• Projections for Q3 and Q4 2025-26 inflation were revised upwards, indicating a cautious stance towards monetary easing.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target of 4% while supporting economic growth.
• Analysts suggest that future rate cuts are unlikely until inflation shows a clear and sustained downward trajectory.

GS3.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

GS3.headline: National Quantum Mission: India Makes Strides in Quantum Computing and Communication
GS3.explainer:
• India's National Quantum Mission (NQM) announced significant progress in developing indigenous quantum computing hardware and secure quantum communication networks.
• Researchers demonstrated a 64-qubit quantum processor prototype, marking a crucial step towards building larger-scale quantum computers.
• Pilot projects for quantum key distribution (QKD) have been successfully implemented in several metropolitan areas, enhancing cybersecurity infrastructure.
• The mission emphasizes collaboration between academia, industry, and government to foster a vibrant quantum technology ecosystem.
• Funding has been allocated for establishing Quantum Technology Hubs across the country to accelerate R&D and skill development.

GS3.trivia: A quantum computer with just 300 qubits could theoretically store more information than the number of atoms in the observable universe.

GS3.headline: PM-Vishwakarma Scheme Expands to Include New Artisanal Trades, Boosts Rural Economy
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) announced the expansion of the PM-Vishwakarma scheme to include additional traditional crafts and trades.
• The scheme provides financial assistance, skill training, access to modern tools, and marketing support to artisans and craftspeople across India.
• The new trades include specific forms of traditional pottery, intricate weaving, and specialized metal crafts, aiming to preserve cultural heritage.
• This expansion is expected to further empower the Vishwakarma community, enhance their income, and integrate them into the formal economy.
• Focus remains on promoting 'Vocal for Local' and 'Made in India' initiatives by strengthening traditional Indian craftsmanship.

GS3.trivia: The term 'Vishwakarma' refers to the divine architect, often associated with craftsmanship and creation in Hindu mythology.

GS3.headline: India Boosts Climate-Resilient Agriculture Initiatives Amidst Erratic Monsoon Patterns
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare announced new measures to promote climate-resilient agriculture practices across vulnerable regions.
• Focus areas include developing drought-resistant crop varieties, precision irrigation techniques, and early warning systems for extreme weather events.
• The initiatives aim to mitigate the impact of increasingly erratic monsoon patterns and ensure food security for the growing population.
• Farmers will receive financial incentives and technical training for adopting sustainable farming methods like conservation tillage and agroforestry.
• Collaboration with agricultural research institutions and international bodies is being strengthened to share best practices and technological innovations.

GS3.headline: National Medical Devices Policy 2023: Progress on Domestic Manufacturing and Export
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare presented an update on the implementation of the National Medical Devices Policy 2023, highlighting increased domestic manufacturing.
• The policy aims to reduce import dependence, promote R&D, and establish India as a global manufacturing hub for medical devices.
• Several new medical device parks have become operational, attracting significant investments from both domestic and international players.
• Regulatory reforms are underway to streamline approval processes and ensure the quality and safety of domestically produced devices.
• India is also focusing on increasing exports of high-quality medical devices to various international markets.

GS3.headline: Namami Gange Mission Enters Phase III: Focus on Tributary Cleaning and Public Participation
GS3.explainer:
• The National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG) announced the commencement of Phase III of the Namami Gange programme, extending its scope.
• The new phase places increased emphasis on the rejuvenation of Ganga's tributaries, including the Yamuna, Gomti, and Kosi, which contribute significantly to pollution.
• Greater public participation and community engagement will be fostered through localized initiatives and citizen science projects.
• Advanced wastewater treatment technologies and bioremediation techniques will be deployed to tackle industrial and municipal pollution.
• The mission aims for a holistic approach, integrating sanitation, biodiversity conservation, and sustainable river basin management.

GS3.trivia: The Ganga river basin is the most populous river basin in the world, home to over 500 million people.

GS3.headline: India Accelerates Border Infrastructure Development in North-Eastern States
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs announced accelerated plans for border infrastructure development along India's North-Eastern frontiers.
• Projects include construction of all-weather roads, strategic bridges, and advanced surveillance systems to enhance security and connectivity.
• The move aims to bolster India's strategic presence, facilitate trade, and integrate remote border areas with the national mainstream.
• Special focus is on improving connectivity to Bhutan, Myanmar, and Bangladesh borders to foster regional economic cooperation.
• The initiative is part of a broader strategy to strengthen border management and counter cross-border illegal activities.

GS3.headline: Government Consults Stakeholders for India's AI Ethics and Governance Framework
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has initiated a broad consultation process for developing India's Artificial Intelligence (AI) ethics and governance framework.
• The framework aims to ensure responsible and ethical development and deployment of AI technologies, addressing concerns like bias, privacy, and accountability.
• Discussions involve experts from academia, industry, civil society, and legal fields to create a comprehensive and future-proof policy.
• The focus is on promoting 'AI for All' while safeguarding fundamental rights and preventing potential misuse of AI.
• This move aligns with global efforts to establish norms and standards for AI governance.

GS3.headline: India Inaugurates New Mega Solar Energy Parks, Boosts Renewable Capacity
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy announced the inauguration of several new mega solar energy parks across states like Rajasthan, Gujarat, and Karnataka.
• These parks collectively add gigawatts of solar power capacity to the national grid, significantly advancing India's renewable energy targets.
• The projects utilize advanced photovoltaic technology and include provisions for energy storage solutions to ensure grid stability.
• The development aligns with India's commitment to reducing its carbon footprint and achieving its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement.
• The new parks are expected to create local employment opportunities and attract further investment in the green energy sector.
